docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I am a researcher in behavior economics, applied microeconomics and natural language processing, with a focus on analyzing the impact of public policies with an economic perspective. I've been working with text datasets of lawcases, public finance, electoral and government database, observing the behavior according to specific topics.

I graduated with my PhD in Business Economics in 2023 and since I've been working with many topics in public policy, judicial behavior, behavior economics and data science. Across my research, the statistical framework and information extraction with natural language processing are the main topics in common.

Nowadays I'm in my post doctoral project at Insper, in the Center for Regulation and Democracy (CRD), which I'm also affiliated.

docs.title: Prosecutorial Independence: a case study of the head of Brazil's public prosecutor's office (PGR)
docs.text:
Conference of Empirical Legal Studies Global Brazil 2026 São Paulo, Brazil · June 2026

Autores: Prado, Mariana Mota & Kerche, Fabio & Wang, Henrique

This paper examines how public prosecutor behave towards politicians, according to the way they are appointed.

Presentation Type Full Article Presentation Pages 25

docs.title: Voice of the population and judge’s sensibility
docs.text:
Conference of Empirical Legal Studies 2024 Atlanta, USA · November 2024

Autores: De Azevendo, Paulo Furquim & Wang, Henrique

Presented the results of the hypothesis of anchoring effect of judicial cases related to corruption. The paper observe two information shocks related to the Carwash Operation, which unveiled a national scandal involving corruption among public officials. Is observed statistically the anchoring effect of those events in judicial cases about corruption but unrelated to the facts disclosed by the Carwash Operation.

Presentation Type Full Article Poster Pages 20 Session Poster Section

docs.title: When in Rome… do less knowledgeable judges mimic their peers?
docs.text:
Conference of Empirical Legal Studies Global Brazil 2024 São Paulo, Brazil · June 2024

Autores: De Azevendo, Paulo Furquim & Wang, Henrique

This paper examines how judges anchor their decisions on their peers’ judgments. It take advantage of the random distribution of judicial cases among the judges in each district and the semi-random migration of judges between districts. Is observed statistically the difference in behavior of the new judges compared to those that already are in that district in terms of decision text and the case outcome.

Presentation Type Full Article Presentation Pages 20

docs.title: When in Rome… do less knowledgeable judges mimic their peers?

docs.title: Workshops Presentations
docs.text: Here are the references of my previous workshop presentations.

docs.title: Introduction to empirical studies with legal dataset
docs.text:
Workshop of empirical analysis of legal dataset · Insper, São Paulo August de 2024 ·

Apresentação introdutória cobrindo tokenização, análise de sentimentos e modelagem de tópicos com exemplos em português. Parte de um workshop de dois dias organizado pelo LabHD-USP.

Tópicos abordados:

  • O que é PLN e para que serve nas humanidades
  • Ferramentas sem código: Voyant Tools, AntConc, NLTK via Google Colab
  • Estudo de caso: análise de corpus de jornais do século XIX
  • Hands-on: classificação de sentimentos em textos literários
